source: axios/axios GitHub Issue #10636 (official postmortem, March 31 2026); CISA alert 2026/04/20
notes: Lead maintainer's npm publishing credentials stolen via RAT delivered through targeted social engineering; attacker manually published this version, bypassing the project's OIDC trusted-publisher flow. Fixed/safe version: axios@1.14.0.

source: Microsoft Security Blog (Apr 1 2026); StepSecurity technical analysis (Mar 31 2026)
notes: Injected as a new, never-imported dependency of the malicious axios versions; its postinstall hook was the actual RAT dropper for macOS/Windows/Linux, while axios's own source was left unmodified.
Decoy/seed version published ~18 hours before the malicious 4.2.1 release, from a separate throwaway npm account, to build clean publishing history and reduce automated-scanner suspicion.
